title: Jason M Turcotte
content:
## About Me

Jason Turcotte (Ph.D., Louisiana State University) is an Associate Professor and Chair of the Communication Department. He received his B.A. in Communication and Political Science from Roger Williams University, and his M.A. in Media, Culture, and Communication from New York University. He teaches primarily in the Multimedia Journalism option, is a former faculty advisor to *The Poly Post,*and co-leads a Media & Politics Research Team.

Prior to teaching, Professor Turcotte spent several years working as a journalist for various print and online media. His research focuses on how the news media cover campaigns and policy issues. Of particular interest is understanding how news norms and processes influence the agenda of key campaign events, including electoral debates.

***Professor Turcotte teaches:***

* News Practicum
* Adv. News Practicum
* Reporting
* Survey of Mass Communication
* Political Communication

title: Jason M Turcotte
content:
## Research

The following is a sample of Professor Turcotte's publications. To learn more about his research and collaborative student research teams visit [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=4IlDAlkAAAAJ&hl=en) or email jmturcotte@cpp.edu

* Turcotte, J. (2023). [*Comedy, cameos, and campaign communication: Leveraging entertainment media to win elections and advance policy*](https://www.routledge.com/Comedy-Cameos-and-Campaign-Communication-Leveraging-Entertainment-Media-to-Win-Elections-and-Advance-Policy/Turcotte/p/book/9781032429052). New York: Routledge (Taylor & Francis).
* Furey, L., Turcotte, J., Corrales, N. J., Frisan, E. N., Sorrell, J., & Urbina Villacorta, N. A. (2023). [Miss (ing) representation: Examining how race shaped news coverage of missing women surrounding Gabby Petito’s disappearance](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/17512786.2023.2259378). *Journalism Practice*.
* Turcotte, J., & Hernandez Florez, N. (2023). How journalistic objectivity turns matters of science into matters of politics: A closer look at climate change and COVID-19 reporting. In H. Schmidt (ed.), [*Issues in Contemporary American Journalism*](https://www.routledge.com/Issues-in-Contemporary-American-Journalism/Schmidt/p/book/9781032325514). Routledge.
* Turcotte, J., Furey, L., Garcia-Ortega, O., Hernandez, N., Siccion, C., & Stephenson, E. (2021).[The novelty news frame: How social identity influences policy attention of minority presidential candidates](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/0739532921990146). *Newspaper Research Journal*, 42(1), 95-110.
* Turcotte, J. (2018). Enemies of the people: Elites, attacks, and news trust in the era of Trump. In A. Hayes (ed.), [*Communication and media in the age of Trump: Assessing campaign coverage, social media use, and political rhetoric*](https://www.peterlang.com/document/1056364). New York: Peter Lang.
* Turcotte, J. (2017). [Predicting policy: Exploring the determinants of issues-based agendas in electoral debates](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/00909882.2017.1382709). *Journal of Applied Communication Research*, 45(5), 576-595.
* Turcotte, J. (2017). [Who’s citing whom? Source selection and elite indexing in electoral debates](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/1077699015625883). *Journalism & Mass Communication Quarterly*, 94(1), 238-258.
* Turcotte, J., Kirzinger, A., Dunaway, J., & Goidel, R. K. (2017). [The many layers of local: Proximity and market influence on the news coverage of the Deepwater Horizon oil spill](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/ssqu.12440). *Social Science Quarterly*, 98(3), 993-1009.
* Turcotte, J., Medenilla, K., Villasenor, K., & Lampwalla, S. (2017).[All the polling (data) that’s fit to print: An analysis of online news coverage of 2016 primary polls](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/08824096.2017.1282856). *Communication Research Reports*, 34(3), 191-200.
* Turcotte, J., York, C., Scholl, R., Irving, J., & Pingree, R. (2015). [News recommendations from social media opinion leaders: Effects on media trust and information seeking](https://academic.oup.com/jcmc/article/20/5/520/4067592). *Journal of Computer-Mediated Communication*, 20(5), 520-535.
* Turcotte, J., & Paul, N. (2015). [A case of more is less: The role of gender in US presidential debates](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/1065912915605581). *Political Research Quarterly*, 68(4), 773-784.
* Turcotte, J. (2015). [The news norms and values of presidential debate agendas: An analysis of format and moderator influence on question content](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/15205436.2014.893362). *Mass Communication & Society*, 18(3), 239-258.
* Turcotte, J., & Goidel, R. K. (2014). [Political knowledge and exposure to the 2012 U.S. presidential debates. Does debate format matter?](https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/ps-political-science-and-politics/article/abs/political-knowledge-and-exposure-to-the-2012-us-presidential-debates-does-debate-format-matter/D77D7BABA8DDB6FA293AAC05368AA0AD) *PS: Political Science & Politics*, 47(2), 449-453.

title: John P. Lloyd
content:
## About Me

Dr. Lloyd is a professor of history at Cal Poly Pomona, where he teaches courses on the Civil War and Reconstruction; the Gilded Age and Progressive Era; the Depression and New Deal; American Constitutional history; labor history; Transportation and Environmental history; and research methods.  Dr. Lloyd has been an advisor for several Teaching American History (TAH) programs in local school districts.

Dr. Lloyd earned his Ph.D. in History from the Claremont Graduate University in 2000.  His dissertation research explored the growth of ideas about race, civil rights, and equal citizenship after the Civil War.  He has published numerous articles and book reviews on a variety of topics in post-Civil War US history, and more recently has published articles on the evolution of transportation and public space in 20th century US urban environments.

 Since 2016, Dr. Lloyd has been involved in efforts to improve and encourage sustainable transportation alternatives at Cal Poly Pomona and surrounding communities.  He currently serves on the Academic Senate, chairs the Senate Budget Committee, and is co-chair of the Alternative Transportation Committee.

title: Jane R. Ballinger

## About Me

Jane Ballinger (Ph.D., University of Texas) is a Professor of Communication. She teaches in the Multimedia Journalism and Public Relations options.

Professionally, Professor Ballinger has worked as a reporter, copy editor and was a public relations consultant for non-profit organizations. In 2006, she was named the outstanding advisor for the College of Letters, Arts, and Social Sciences at Cal Poly Pomona.

***Professor Ballinger teaches:***

* Media Design
* Media, Politics, Sex, & Violence
* Reporting
* Public Opinion, Propaganda, and the Mass Media
* Political Economy of Mass Communication
* Media Criticism

title: Jun Myers
content:
## About Me

Dr. Jun Myers received her Ph.D. in Mass Communication at the University of Minnesota, Twin Cities. Her specialty areas include advertising, promotions, integrated marketing communication, e-marketing and business research methods. Dr. Myers teaches both undergraduate and M.B.A. classes, focusing on real-world application of theory, as well as connecting with local marketing agencies and businesses, to bring a well-rounded approach to the classroom. Dr. Myers’ research centers on consumers’ cognition, emotion, motivation, attitude and behavior issues in both for-profit and nonprofit contexts, to facilitate “insight-based” managerial decision making and solving real-world problems. Dr. Myers frequently presents her research and serves as a reviewer for professional communities such as *American Academy of Advertising, American Marketing Association, Marketing Educators’ Association, Direct Marketing Association’s Research Summit and International Journal of Advertising.* Dr. Myers’ work appears in journals such as *Journal of Marketing Communication, Journal of Current Issues and Research in Advertising, Journal of Advertising Education, Journal of Communication Management*, and *International Journal of Integrated Marketing Communication*.

Dr. Myers serves as a CPP Academic Senator for College of Business Administration, Senate Budget Committee, and a number of colledge and department committees.

title: Juanita P. Roxas
content:
## About Me

Dr. Roxas is the department chair and professor of international business and marketing at Cal Poly Pomona. She received her Ph.D. (major in marketing and minor in international business) from the University of Georgia (1992). Her research interests include cross-cultural studies in consumer behavior, interactive marketing and international marketing strategy. She has published in such journals as the  International Marketing Review, Journal of Advertising Education, Competition Forum andJournal of Global Marketing . She is a member of the graduate faculty and teaches courses in the areas of Global Marketing Strategy, International Marketing, Marketing Research, Services Marketing and Marketing Management. Dr. Roxas also serves as academic adviser for students majoring in international business. Moreover, she is the recipient of two prestigious awards in the College of Business Administration: Faculty of the Year (2007) and Advisor of the Year (2010).

title: Juanita P. Roxas
content:
## Selected Publications

* Debbora Whitson, Ozkaya, H. Erkan, Juanita Roxas (Forthcoming), “Changes in Consumer Segments and Preferences to Green Labeling” *International Journal of Consumer Studies*.
* Helena Czepiec, Frank Bryant, Juanita Roxas and Debbora Whitson (2012), “Teaching Students How To Create Viral Advertising/Marketing: A Case Study,” *Journal of Advertising Education*, 16 (1).
* Jun Myers, Helena Czepiec, Juanita Roxas and Debbora Whitson (2011), “Teaching Students Self Advertising/Marketing Skills in the Age of Social Media: Designing An Experiential Exercise,” *Journal of Advertising Education*, 15 (1), 23-29.
* Juanita P. Roxas and Sandra M. Huszagh (1996), “Adjusting to Economic Change in Developing Countries,” *Journal of Global Marketing*, 9 (3).
* Sandra M. Huszagh, Juanita P. Roxas and Kay L. Keck (1992), “Marketing Practices in the Changing Philippine Macroeconomic Environment,” *International Marketing Review*, 9 (1), 32-43.

title: Javad Seif
content:
## Background

**Dr. Javad Seif** ([hear pronounciation](https://namedrop.io/javadseif)) is an Associate Professor in the Department of Industrial and Manufacturing Engineering at Cal Poly Pomona. He earned his Ph.D. in Industrial and Systems Engineering from the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, an M.S. from the University of Tehran, and a B.S. from the University of Tabriz. He also holds a Graduate Certificate in Reliability and Maintainability Engineering from the University of Tennessee, a Certificate in Model-Based Systems Engineering from Caltech, and professional certifications as a **Certified Reliability Engineer (CRE)** and **Certified Quality Engineer (CQE)** from ASQ, along with **MBTI® Step I™/II™** practitioner certification.

Dr. Seif’s research focuses on **optimization, system thinking, reliability, and data-driven decision-making**. He develops mathematical models and computational methods for applications in flight and maintenance planning, quality and reliability engineering, production scheduling, maintenance planning, physical asset management, and fleet replacement. His work has been published in leading peer-reviewed journals and conference proceedings and is available on [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=iVA0Yb8AAAAJ&hl=en).

At Cal Poly Pomona, Dr. Seif teaches undergraduate and graduate courses including **Statistical Quality Control, Reliability Engineering, Operations Research, Advanced Methods in Operations Research, and Systems Science**. He has supervised numerous student research and design projects that integrate analytics, optimization, and applied problem-solving.

He is an active member of professional societies including **IISE, INFORMS, ASQ,** and **INCOSE**, and serves as a reviewer for journals in industrial engineering and operations research.

title: Javad Seif
content:
## Education

2018 Ph.D., Industrial and Systems Engineering, University of Tennessee, Knoxville, TN

2013 M.S., Industrial Engineering, University of Tehran, Tehran, Iran

2008 B.S., Industrial Engineering (Concentration in Manufacturing), University of Tabriz, Tabriz, Iran

title: Javad Seif
content:
## Certifications

title: Joanne Sohn
content:
## About Me

Dr. Joanne Sohn graduated from UC Berkeley with an Degree in English and from Tufts School of Veterinary Medicine for her DVM. She also has a Masters in Public Health from UC Davis.

Dr. Sohn was the director of the Animal Health Science program at Cal Poly Pomona from 2014-22 and has been a clinical veterinarian for more than twenty-five years. Dr. Sohn has developed multiple hands-on learning opportunities for students and has been heavily involved in experiential learning and the First Year Experience program for students.  Her passion for international travel has resulted in organizing trips with students to Thailand and South Africa. As a result of her numerous grants, the on-campus veterinary teaching lab is equipped with some of the most up-to-date equipment and technology.

title: Juintow Lin
content:
## About Me

Juintow Lin is a Registered Architect and a Professor at Cal Poly Pomona and a founding partner of FoxLin. At Cal Poly Pomona, she teaches design and sustainability courses, and helps to organize the biennial Building Enclosures Sustainability Symposium (BESS) conference, designed to bring together professionals from academia, architecture, engineering and construction, as well as students, to discuss state-of-the-art sustainable building enclosure design.

She created an online community for students and young practitioners to learn about and discuss design tools specifically as they relate to issue of sustainability. Juintow has worked in the offices of Marmol Radziner and Associates in Los Angeles, Foster and Partners in London, and Pei Cobb Freed and Partners in New York. Lin received both her undergraduate and graduate degrees from MIT. She was a pioneering member of the Kinetic Design Group at MIT investigating the design and application of behavioral kinetic systems in architecture. She also served as a Research Fellow at MIT working on Sustainable Urban Housing in China and co-edited and co-authored a book of the same title in 2006.

* [Foxlin Architects](http://www.foxlin.com/)

## Education and Academic Training

**Ph.D.**,  Molecular Biology and Biochemistry, The University of Melbourne, Australia

**Postdoc**,  Cell Biology, Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI), USA

title: Junjun Liu
content:
## Research Interests

This lab investigates the molecular mechanisms governing the regulation of metastasis, with a particular focus on the role of epigenetic modifications. Specifically, we are studying how the ubiquitination and deubiquitination of histone H2A influence the migration and invasion of breast cancer cells. This project is conducted in collaboration with the City of Hope Cancer Research Institute and is supported by funding from the NIH.

![](breast-cancer.jpg)

[Misaligned chromosomes in cells.mp4](misalgnd-chromosme.mp4 "Misaligned chromosomes in cancer cells")

The video above shows our finding that if the activity of deubiquitinase USP16 is impaired, the chromosome alignment during mitosis is significantly affected, which may ultimately cause cancer.

**Dr. Liu received the following awards in recent years:**

Department of Biology Faculty Research Award, 2020

College of Science Ralph W. Ames Distinguished Research Award, 2024

Outstanding Professor voted by Cal Poly Pomona student-athletes, 2024

title: Junjun Liu
content:
## Research Opportunities

Periodically, our lab provides research opportunities to current Cal Poly Pomona undergraduate students ideally with more than one year remaining at the University. Highly motivated students who are interested in biomedical research and/or biotechnology are welcome to contact Dr. Junjun Liu at junjunliu@cpp.edu.

title: John W Morton
content:
## Bio

Dr. John Morton received his Ph.D. in Management from the University of California, Irvine and a B.A. from the University of Hawaii at Manoa. Using an experiential approach, Dr. Morton prepares his students for success in our increasingly dynamic business environment. He primarily teaches courses in Human Resources Management and emphasizes the practical applications of course content. His research focuses primarily on the areas of diversity management, lower skilled workers, workplace mistreatment, and leadership perceptions, with the goal of improving organizations for all employees. Dr. Morton has published his research in internationally recognized peer-reviewed journals, including *Human Resource Management Review*, *Group and Organization Management*, and *Organization Management Journal*. Prior to joining Cal Poly Pomona, Dr. Morton completed a Postdoctoral Fellowship in the College of Business at Colorado State University.

title: Kip A. Dickson
content:
## About Me

Professor Dickson is a tenured professor and has been teaching at Pomona for the past 28 years. He has an undergraduate Bachelor of Architecture from Cal Poly in 1983 and a Master of Architecture II from the Harvard Graduate School of Design.

Kip Dickson teaches in the areas of pre-design, and professional practice, professional ethics and principally in design. He has been the Graduate Coordinator for the Department for the past 10 years. He has 30 years of professional design, management and construction experience in the design of institutional buildings, mixed use developments and custom residential projects. He has a significant history in the design and planning of educational facilities. His professional experience has covered leadership roles in large corporate offices including Dworsky Associates and Perkins & Will. He maintains a private practice DeVA deVeer Dickson Architecture Inc. based in Pasadena CA. The design-build practice has been recognized with honor and merit awards by the American Institute of Architects for both institutional and residential design.

In addition, Kip is currently an active member in AGD American General Design. AGD is s full service Development, Design and Construction organization that develops and builds mixed use transit orientated developments in southern California. This practice feeds his interest in alternate means of project delivery thought in Professional Practice.

Professor Dickson is registered in California and has served as a commissioner to the California Architects Board. Currently he serves as the coordinator for the Cavin Family Traveling Fellowship that awards $10,000 annually to alumni for travel.

### **Special Projects**

Dr. Miller is also involved in year round work with Centennial Farm, a 3-acre working farm located in Costa Mesa, California on the [OC Fair & Event Center](https://ocfair.com/) grounds. She has collaboratively developed agriculture education curriculum for various events and programs at Centennial Farm and is the voice behind the [Cow Milking video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=plRd4xKSxJw), sponsored by the [Dairy Council of California,](https://dairycouncilofca.org/)that visitors watch while visiting the Millennium Barn at the farm.

Dr. Kang received her Ph.D. in Accounting from the University of California, Irvine, and she is a Certified Public Accountant (CPA). She teaches Auditing and Cost Accounting. She has been published in Accounting Horizons, Accounting Research Journal, Auditing: A Journal of Practice & Theory, Business and Society Review, Managerial Auditing Journal, and other high-influence accounting and auditing journals.

title: Kang Hoon Sung
content:
## About Me

Kang Hoon Sung (Ph.D., University of Florida) is a Professor of Communication. He teaches in the Public Relations and Organizational Communication options.

Professor Sung’s research focuses on online strategic communication and corporate social responsibility. He received his B.A. (English Language and Literature) from Korea University and M.A. (Public Relations) and Ph.D. (Mass Communications) from the University of Florida. Professionally, Professor Sung has worked as a PR consultant for various multinational companies such as Boeing, MasterCard, Bacardi and Outback Steakhouse.

***Professor Sung teaches:***

* Communication Theory
* Communication Research
* Persuasion
* PR Case Studies
* PR Campaigns

title: Kevin B. Grell
content:
## About Me

Dr. Kevin Grell is an adjunct faculty teaching Finance at the College of Business. He is the CEO of Los Angeles-based APEN Designs, Inc., a firm specializing in crowdfunding marketing. He teaches RS 4990 (Sustainable Economics) at the Lyle Center.

Grell also teaches a course titled "Real Options and Managerial Incentives to Invest" at the University of Southern Denmark. His research specialty is on the various applications of real options approach in entrepreneurial finances and crowdfunding. He is also a regular contributor to WIRED Magazine.

He received his Ph.D. (business economics) and master's and bachelor's degree (mathematics and economics) from the University of Southern Denmark.

title: Kristen Conway-Gómez, Ph.D.
content:
## About Me

Dr. Conway-Gómez regularly teaches *Regenerative Concepts and Social Practices* (RS 5100) in the MSRS program. Her research interests focus on:

* Natural resource conservation from social and ecological perspectives
* Latin American resource conservation
* Cross-cultural research

Dr. Conway-Gómez received her Bachelor of Arts in Environmental Studies and Religious Studies from Macalester College, and her M.A. in Latin American Studies/Tropical Conservation and Development and Ph.D. in Geography from the University of Florida.

She is a professor of geography in the Department of Geography and Anthropology at the College of Letters, Arts and Social Sciences. Learn more about her teaching and mentoring philosophies and view pictures of her field work at her faculty page [here](http://www.cpp.edu/~kconwaygomez/ "Kristen Conway-Gomez, Ph.D.").

title: Kristen Conway-Gómez, Ph.D.

title: Kyle D. Brown
content:
## Profile

Dr. Brown is a Professor of Landscape Architecture at California State Polytechnic University, Pomona. He previously served as Director of the Lyle Center for Regenerative Studies for 14 years, Interim Dean for the College of Environmental Design, and chair of the University’s Environmental Sustainability Task Force.  Dr. Brown is highly active in the community, having served as a Planning Commissioner for the City of Pomona from 2017-2024, and as a board member of Clean & Green Pomona, a local environmental action group, from 2011-Present.  He has been recognized with numerous awards, including the George P. Hart Award for outstanding faculty leadership, multiple community Service Awards from the City of Pomona, Pomona Unified School District, Clean & Green Pomona, and Community Service and Professional Merit Awards from the American Society for Landscape Architects.

[In 2019, Dr. Brown launched Green City Blog](http://greencityblog.com/), to showcase the work of his Green City Studio at Cal Poly Pomona.

* [Schedule an office hour appointment](https://student-appointments-with-dr-brown.appointlet.com/s/30-minute-office-hour-meeting-face-to-face)

title: Ken Zhang
content:
## Bio

Dr. Qian (Ken) Zhang is an Assistant Professor of Technology and Operations Management at the College of Business Administration at California State Polytechnic University, Pomona. His research interests relate to revenue management, pricing models, platform and information design using Game Theory. In particular, he studies platform's pricing and information provision decision considering strategic behaviors of consumers. In addition, Ken is also interested in machine learning applications in revenue management and finance. He also has experience working as a full-time data scientist in the industry, including at Bloomberg, and has provided project-based consulting services for companies like Two Sigma. He has taught courses in the areas of data analytics and machine learning at both undergraduate and graduate level.

title: Kevin M. Finch
content:
## About Me

Kevin Finch has made a name for himself during his academic career. This Los Angeles native will not only reference the deep tracks of the city on a daily basis, but has also aligned himself with the best designers, thinkers, makers, and doers the region has to offer.

As a son to painters, Kevin has always been involved in the art world. His beginnings as an engineering draftsman and a hobbyist model-maker formed a solid base upon which he could build impressive curriculum vitae. Kevin is a graduate of Cal Poly [BSLA â€˜13] as well as a graduate from the Southern California Institute of Architecture, also known as SCI-Arc [M.Arch I â€˜17].

After having worked at several design offices in the greater Los Angeles area, as well as San Diego and Mexico, Kevin brings a unique view to the traditional approach of design pedagogy. Kevin has built public works in San Diego County and is currently working on a number of professional design competitions with his firm F.N.S.H.

title: Karlyn Griffith
content:
## About Me

Karlyn Griffith is Associate Professor of Art History at Cal Poly Pomona. Her research focuses on the interplay of sacred and secular visual imagery in medieval culture and the reuse of Roman and early medieval materials in medieval architecture. Dr. Griffith has published several studies on late medieval illustrated Apocalypses and recently co-authored *The* *Val-Dieu Apocalypse* with Darron Burrows and Nigel Morgan. Her current book project *The Innovation and Evolution of* *Illuminated Apocalypses in Late Medieval France* firstly identifies and localizes several currently unknown illustrated Apocalypses made in France in the fourteenth century, and secondly demonstrates new perspectives for understanding the reception of not only Apocalypses but also any illustrated manuscript by considering dynamics of place, the role of language, production practices, and the purpose of visual innovation. Research for this project has been supported by the Mellon Foundation, the Institute for Historical Research in London, the American Philosophical Society, and the Bibliographical Society of the UK.

title: Karlyn Griffith
content:
## Publications

BOOKS

*Tributes in Honor of Richard K. Emmerson: Crossing Medieval Disciplines*. Editor with Dierdre Carter and Elina Gertsman. Brepols, 2021.

*The* *Val-Dieu Apocalypse*. Facsimile commentary for London, British Library MS 17333. With Daron Burrows and Nigel Morgan. M. Moliero, 2020.

JOURNAL ARTICLES

“Adaptation and Assemblage: The Fourteenth-Century *Antichrist Compilation* as a Courtly Manual for Salvation,” *Viator* 48 (2017): 191-232.

“The Codicological Histories of Two Metz Compilations (MSS Douce 308 and Harley 4972) and the Implications of Owner Manipulations,” *Pecia* 17 (2016): 93-127.

“Performative Reading and Receiving a Performance of the *Jour du Jugement* in MS Besançon 579,” *Comparative Drama* 45 (2011): 99-126.

“Viewing the Romance of Antichrist in the Miniatures of the *Jour du Jugement*, MS Besançon 579,” *Athanor* 27 (2009): 25-33.

BOOK CHAPTERS

“The Spectacle of Violence and Romance in Three English, Metrical Apocalypses,” in *Tributes in Honor of Richard K. Emmerson: Crossing Medieval Disciplines*, ed. Dierdre Carter, Elina Gertsman, and Karlyn Griffith (Brepols, 2021), 173-194.

“Personalized Eschatology and Lorraine Apocalypses, ca. 1295-1320,” in *Catastrophes and the Apocalyptic*, ed. Robert E. Bjork (Brepols, 2019), 29-52.

REVIEWS

“Review of The Crusader Bible: A Gothic Masterpiece, Blanton Museum of Art at the University of Texas, Austin,” co-authored with Mickey Abel, *caa.review* (September 1, 2016), http://www.caareviews.org/reviews/3078#.V\_GTZ5MrLJG

title: Kathryn M McCulloch
content: ## Research Interests

title: Kathryn M McCulloch
content:
### Overview

Organisms have not evolved in a vacuum – instead, they have interacted with their environment, competed for resources with other species, and collaborated with other organisms to thrive. These various forces have led to the evolution of complex pathways that produce signaling molecules, secondary metabolites, or otherwise provide the organism with an evolutionary advantage. To date, these pathways offer both the opportunity to develop new therapies, such as antibiotics, and offer potential targets for defeating pathogens or promoting human health. The McCulloch group aims to understand the chemistry of enzymes found within specific pathways. Currently, we are studying oxidoreductases (enzymes that catalyze either oxidations or reductions) encoded within the bile acid induced operon of some gut bacteria. We use a recombinant approach to overexpress each protein, and then use a combination of X-ray crystallography and *in vitro* biochemical assays to develop a molecular understanding of their structures and chemical reactivities.

![Interactions between species](interspecies-interactions.jpg)

title: Kathryn M McCulloch
content:
### Bile Acid Metabolism

Our group is interested in understanding the transformation of primary bile acids - molecules synthesized in the liver and essential in the absorption of fat-soluble vitamins and nutrients, as well as important signaling molecules - into secondary bile acids by some species of gut bacteria. We use a recombinant approach to overexpress putative enzymes from this metabolic pathway and aim to determine the three-dimensional structures of these enzymes to answer important questions about how they work.

title: Kwon Gi Mun
content:
## About Me

Dr. Mun’s research interest lies in supply chain management and energy interface, as well as healthcare and healthcare operations. He has experience working with industry and government partners, especially in energy policy /energy system modeling/healthcare operations/disease prediction using data analytics. His work has appeared in *Production and Operations Management* and *Manufacturing & Service Operations Management*. Dr. Mun received his Ph.D. degree in both Operations Research and Business (Supply Chain Management) as the Joint Degree from Rutgers University in 2016 and the M.A. in Economics from University of Missouri Columbia in 2009 and he, as a doctoral student, also studied Operations Research in IEOR at Columbia University. Dr. Mun was a full-time staff at Korean Chamber of Commerce and Industry, Seoul, Korea.

## Bio

After practicing law as a commercial litigator for many years, Kevin Farmer earned a doctorate in Organization Studies before joining the HR emphasis faculty. In his teaching and research, he focuses on Employment Law, Labor Relations and Alternative Dispute Resolution as well as Organizational Justice and the development of teaching techniques. He is qualified as a Senior Certified Professional by the Society for Human Resource Management and has earned a credential in California Employment Law from that organization.

## About Me

Sasha Plotnikova is a designer living and working in Los Angeles. After studying art history and urban studies at McGill University, Sasha earned an M. Arch from Rice University. Her design practice ranges from visual identity and book design projects to residential remodels, additions, and backyard homes. She has written essays on the commodification of housing and the privatization of public space for publications including Failed Architecture, MONU, and the Law & Political Economy Project blog.

title: Katrin Terstegen
content:
## About Me

Katrin Terstegen is principal of Los Angeles based design studio KATRINTERSTEGEN, a registered architect in California, and Associate Professor in the Architecture Department at California Polytechnic State University, Pomona. She has taught at the Technical University Berlin, the University of Auckland in New Zealand, and the University of California in Los Angeles, served as juror on reviews at numerous international universities, and has lectured internationally on her work. She served on the board of directors of the non-profit organization Los Angeles Forum for Architecture and Urban Design, where she was President in 2019.

Prior to establishing her firm, she worked at Johnston Marklee for ten years, at Pugh+Scarpa (now Brook+Scarpa) in Los Angeles, and at firms in Paris and Amsterdam. Terstegen holds architecture degrees from the Technical University Berlin and the University of Westminster in London and also studied architecture at the ETSAB in Barcelona. In her research and teaching, she examines architectural typology and the language of materials and construction. At Cal Poly Pomona, she currently teaches Second Year Studio, Senior Projects, and Professional Electives.

[www.katrinterstegen.com](https://www.katrinterstegen.com)

title: Keiji Uesugi
content:
## About Me

Keiji Uesugi is an assistant professor in the Landscape Architecture Department. He is a licensed landscape architect with over 20 years of experience with his own professional practice.
He is an expert in cultural landscapes and Japanese gardens of North America. As part of the faculty at Cal Poly Pomona University, Keiji has taught undergraduate and graduate courses including design, construction, history of Asian gardens, and ethnic studies.
Keiji fosters cultural enrichment in the community as the director for the Japanese drumming music ensemble Yoki Daiko at the Tenrikyo Church in Boyle Heights, CA. He also performs Japanese court music Gagaku, which has over 2000 years of music history.

title: Lori A. Barker
content:
## About Me

Dr. Lori A. Barker received her B.A. in psychology from Yale University and her Ph.D. in clinical psychology from UCLA. After receiving her degree, Dr. Barker spent one additional year at UCLA as a National Institute of Mental Health postdoctoral research fellow.

Dr. Barker’s primary area of interest is multicultural psychology, and she is the co-author of an undergraduate textbook on that subject titled, *Multicultural Psychology: Understanding Our Diverse Communities* (Oxford University Press). She is also the author of *Obama on Our Minds: The Impact of Obama on the Psyche of America* (Oxford University Press)*.*Her research focuses on multicultural attitudes, factors that influence the effectiveness of multicultural education and diversity training programs, and the psychological impact of representation (e.g., diverse presidential candidates). Dr. Barker regularly serves as a diversity, equity and inclusion consultant for corporations, community, and professional organizations. She is also a licensed clinical psychologist with a private practice where she specializes in providing culturally sensitive, responsive, inclusive, and affirming therapy.

Dr. Barker is a past president of the Western Psychological Association (WPA), a fellow of the organization, and past recipient of their Outstanding Teaching Award. Other awards include Outstanding Advisor, Psi Chi Western Region; the Jose A. Gomez Faculty Excellence Award, Cal Poly Pomona, College of Letters Arts and Social Sciences; Outstanding Advisor, Office of Student Life, Cal Poly Pomona; Professor of the Year, Psi Chi, Cal Poly Pomona Chapter; and Diversity Champion, Cal Poly Pomona.

title: Lisa A. Brownfield
content:
## About Me

Lisa Brownfield is a practicing urban planner, specializing in advanced policy planning. She has over 30 years of professional experience in both the public and private sectors. Ms. Brownfield is based in Pasadena CA; her clients are located throughout the western US but are primarily in California.  Lisa has an extensive expertise in long-term visionary planning (general plans, specific plans), housing, and community engagement. She is well versed in entitlement processes, development/zoning codes, environmental review, and project management.  Ms. Brownfield has authored award winning comprehensive plans, visionary planning documents, and specific plans for clients throughout California.

As a lecturer, Lisa focuses on providing students with technical knowledge and practical skills enabling them to become enthusiastic and integral planning team members immediately upon graduation and employment.

Lisa loves the urban environment; she is an admitted “map geek”. She believes a planner’s role is to help each city’s residents determine the type of place they want to live, work, and play and then turn that vision into reality.

In addition to her Cal Poly and professional planning responsibilities, Lisa is a docent at Pasadena’s historic and “art as architecture” landmark, the Gamble House.

Ms. Brownfield holds a Master of Art, Architecture and Urban Planning from UCLA and a Bachelor of Science, Urban Planning from Cal Poly Pomona.

title: Lauren D Furey
content:
## Faculty Bio

Lauren Furey (Ph.D., University of Florida) is an Associate Professor of Communication in the Communication Department. She teaches in the multimedia journalism option, advises [The Poly Post](https://thepolypost.com) student newspaper and [The Poly Pulse](https://polypost.wpenginepowered.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/PolyPulse2025Magazine-1-2.pdf) magazine, as well as co-leads a Media and Politics Research Team with undergraduate students. Outside of teaching, she researches the portrayal of women, particularly in politics, by the news media and in popular culture and co-hosts a podcast featuring interviews with women politicians called [The Pink State](https://open.spotify.com/show/02KE9vSfDrnGLonB1kJV0l?si=0a0737825b9f411c&nd=1&dlsi=c4db3cf5dd2e415a).

Prior to becoming a professor, Dr. Furey worked as a journalist and has published in newspapers like the Jacksonville Business Journal, The Florida Times-Union, Lubbock Avalanche Journal, Daily News Egypt, Daily Star Egypt, and Pakistan Christian Post. She also wrote for New York Times and Wall Street Journal editors and columnists while completing her master's in journalism from Columbia Journalism School. She received her B.A. in communications, with a concentration in journalism, from the University of North Florida.

title: Lauren D Furey
content:
## Sample Publications

**Furey, L. D.**, & Hall, A. E. (2025). She’s no “lady president”: Exploring the evolution of women presidents in TV and movies. In T. Everbach, G. Nisbett, & P. Newly (Eds.), *[Women’s leadership in popular culture](https://a.co/d/ek81Vss).* Lexington Books.

Hall, A. E., & **Furey, L. D.** (2025). I’m with her: Exploring Barbie for President campaign narratives with young women and moms. In R. Hains & E. Aguilo-Perez (Eds.), [*#Barbie and social media: Digital discourses and Mattel’s celebrity doll*](https://a.co/d/3Rkcyn7). Palgrave Macmillan.

Horowitz, N., Peterson, H., & **Furey, L. D.** (2024). Exploring the media discourse of Neurodiversity and Neurodivergent individuals: A step toward discovering possibilities for all people. *Western Journal of Communication, 89*(4), 654-674. <https://doi.org/10.1080/10570314.2024.2395314> **\*This paper features a Cal Poly Pomona undergraduate student co-author.**

**Furey, L. D.**, Turcotte, J., Sorrell, J., Urbina, N., Frisan, E., & Corrales, N. (2023). Miss(ing) representation: Examining how race shaped news coverage of missing women surrounding Gabby Petito's disappearance. *Journalism Practice, 19*(7), 1538-1555. <https://doi.org/10.1080/17512786.2023.2259378> **\*This paper features four Cal Poly Pomona undergraduate student co-authors.**

Hall, A. E., **Furey, L. D.**, & Muttram, L. (2023). The rise of #GirlDad in a #BoyMom world: Exploring Instagram's role in influencing performative parenthood. *Journal of Social Media in Society, 12*(1), 235-256. **\*This paper features a Cal Poly Pomona undergraduate student co-author.**

Turcotte, J., **Furey, L. D.**, Garcia-Ortega, J. M., Hernandez, N., Siccion, C., & Stephenson, E. (2021). ALaureen Hom received her PhD in Planning, Policy, and Design with an emphasis in Asian American Studies at the University of California, Irvine and an MPH in Sociomedical Sciences, Urbanism and the Built Environment concentration at the Mailman School of Public Health at Columbia University. Her research and teaching interests are at the intersection of public policy, urban studies, and ethnic studies.

title: Laureen D. Hom
content:
## Academic Area

Public Policy, Urban Studies, Ethnic Studies

title: Laureen D. Hom
content:
## Areas of Expertise

Urban and social policies, race, ethnic communities and spaces, civic engagement, collaborative governance

title: Laureen D. Hom
content:
## Research Statement

Laureen D. Hom received a PhD in Planning, Policy, and Design with an emphasis in Asian American Studies and an MPH with a specialization in Sociomedical Sciences and Urbanism and the Built Environment. As an interdisciplinary scholar, her research and teaching is at the intersection of urban studies, Ethnic Studies, public policy, and public administration. Her primary expertise is in Asian American politics and places, and her work broadly examines community formations, grassroots organizations and organizing, and political representation in ethnic spaces in Southern California. She has published on gentrification politics in Los Angeles Chinatown; the growth of Asian American and Pacific Islander communities across Orange County, California; and the history of the Chinese Hospital in San Francisco Chinatown. She also has professional experience in program management, evaluation, and community-engaged collaborative research in health services and non-profit organizations serving unhoused and immigrant communities in San Francisco and New York.

title: Laureen D. Hom
content:
## Academic Degrees

* PhD, University of California, Irvine, 2018
* MPH, Columbia University, 2008
* BA, University of California, Los Angeles, 2004

title: Lyle D. Janicek
content:
## About Me

Lyle Janicek is a Senior Regional Planner in the Sustainable and Resilient Development Department at the Southern California Association of Governments. Lyle Joined SCAG in 2018 and served as a technical assistance lead for integrating regional land use strategies in the 2020 Regional Transportation Plan/Sustainable Communities Strategy, better known as Connect SoCal 2020.

His work includes: collaborating with universities and research/planning agencies regionally and nationally, producing SCAG’s Priority Development Areas (PDAs) for Connect SoCal 2024, developing the accessibility analysis and health implications assessment for the environmental justice technical report, programming several million dollars in Regional Early Action Planning (REAP) funding into housing supportive projects throughout Southern California.

Lyle received his master’s degree in urban and Regional Planning from Cal Poly Pomona in 2019. His thesis “An Evaluation of Projected Versus Actual Ridership on Los Angeles’ Metro Rail Lines” focused on how ridership performed in Metro’s rail network. Lyle received his bachelor’s degree in landscape architecture from the University of Nebraska, Lincoln, in May of 2015. Born and raised in Kimball, a one-stoplight town in Western Nebraska, Lyle takes pride in having a small-town perspective in a global society.

title: Elektra Grant
content:
## About Me

Elektra Grant (she/her) offers a unique understanding of the relationship between environmentally beneficial projects, and quality of life in communities. Her areas of expertise are sustainability education and consulting, ecologically-informed systems, social sustainability, facilities inspections, green building strategies, sustainability in higher ed, and sustainability in the arts.

Elektra's evolving path in her sustainability commitment has led to the Transition movement, which was originally conceived as grassroots groups "promot[ing] community-driven responses to peak oil that focus on cooperative effort and meet basic needs as sustainably and close to home as possible.” Along those lines, Elektra lived in a sustainability-focused intentional community, modeled after the systems and teachings of the Lyle Center for Regenerative Studies.  Currently a board member of the Westwood Greenway, Elektra's previous service includes involvement with Otis College of Art and Design’s Environmental Steering Committee, USGBC-LA’s Regional Partners Committee, the Living Building Challenge - LA Collaborative, and the Municipal Green Building Conference & Expo.

Elektra holds a Master of Science in Regenerative Studies from Cal Poly Pomona, and a Bachelor of Fine Arts in Film, Video and Animation from Rhode Island School of Design.  She is a LEED Green Associate and Certified Sustainable Building Advisor.  [LinkedIn](https://nam11.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fin%2Felektragrant%2F&data=04%7C01%7Csbgonzaga%40cpp.edu%7Ca5eacc5da06346edb27a08d9df6e93f9%7C164ba61e39ec4f5d89ffaa1f00a521b4%7C0%7C0%7C637786488289511548%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000&sdata=%2BJM7ytmYQrrLMerP8WPb5R5hrGnowSuRBUX0KRMme2g%3D&reserved=0 "Original URL: https://www.linkedin.com/in/elektragrant/. Click or tap if you trust this link.")

title: Lois E Hoyt
content:
## About Me

Ms. Hoyt brings over 30 years' human resources management in driving people initiatives for various Fortune 100 and 500 companies, global, non-profit, and union organizations, start-ups, publicly-traded and privately-held employers. Ms. Hoyt’s expertise includes optimizing teams through workforce management and engagement efforts, and reducing workplace conflict by creating sustainable programs that build and retain organizational excellence. Ms. Hoyt has developed necessary infrastructures that allow start-up companies to grow, and has led high-impact change initiatives for fusing diverse work cultures and preserving high performing talent in large corporate cultures during stressful mergers, acquisitions, and private equity takeovers. Among Lois’ areas of HR expertise, it is her practical business guidance, leadership coaching, and employment law knowledge that organizations trust most to confidentially navigate risk while leveraging resources that spark growth. For the past 22 years, Ms. Hoyt has also passionately taught part-time at various colleges and universities, successfully integrating tried theories with practical application to encourage the next generation of progressive business leaders to stand firm on a foundation of ethics, to embrace a nimble, yet strong business acumen, and above all else, to exude passion for leading others to success.

title: Luis Hoyos
content:
## About Me

Luis Hoyos is a licensed architect, urban designer and preservationist. He is the founder of Luis Hoyos Architects, Inc. and is Professor at the Department of Architecture in the College of Environmental Design at Cal Poly Pomona. His designs for adapted historic structures such as the Point Fermin Lighthouse, the Cabrillo Beach Bathhouse and El Pueblo de Los Angeles Buildings and Plaza have won professional recognition.

Hoyos's interests are mainly in the areas of urban design and historic preservation with an emphasis on the training of designers for the practice. He is a former and current Commissioner on the State Historic Resources Commission, where he served as Chair for two years. He is also a former member of the Board of Directors of the Los Angeles Conservancy and an Advisor and member of the Board of Trustees of the National Trust for Historic Preservation. Hoyos regularly collaborates on preservation projects with the National Park Service, such as the HABS Project on The Forty Acres in Delano, Ca., and the American Latino Heritage Initiative Theme Study (currently published online) where he acted as national co-chair for the study.

He was recently appointed by President Obama to the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation, a body that advises Congress and the President on matters pertaining to the preservation of historical resources at a national scale. In addition, Mr. Hoyos is a member of the National Park System Advisory Board's Landmarks Committe which reviews nominations to the National Historic Landmarks Program and serves to craft preservation policy nationwide.

Hoyos holds a Bachelor of Architecture degree from the Universidad Autonoma de Guadalajara (1977) and a Master's Degree in Urban Design from the Graduate School of Design at Harvard University (1983).

* [Member of the National Park System Advisory Board Landamrks Committee](http://nps.gov/)
* [Member of the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation](http://achp.org/)
* [NPS Theme Study: The American Latino and the Making of the US](https://www.nps.gov/heritageinitiatives/latino/latinothemestudy/)
* [Cal Poly horiz for applic.doc](https://cpp-env-static.s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/prod/s3fs-public/attachments/user/Cal%20Poly%20horiz%20for%20applic.doc)

content:
## Bio

* Dr. Liang Sun is an Assistant Professor of Finance in the College of Business Administration at Cal Poly Pomona. Her research focuses on empirical corporate finance and mutual funds. Liang’s most recent research shows that pilot CEOs are associated with higher intensity of FinTech adoption. Her other papers cover a wide range of topics, from board structure to mutual fund side-by-side management.
* Prior to joining Cal Poly Pomona, Liang worked at Illinois Wesleyan University and earned her Ph.D. in Finance from the University of North Texas.

title: Lucas Krusinski
content:
### **Biography**

Dr. Krusinski is a multidisciplinary scientist interested in the effects of agricultural practices on food nutrient density and animal welfare. He is currently an Assistant Professor of Animal Science - Biotechnology, and his work bridges multiple disciplines including food science, animal science, metabolomics, grazing management, molecular biology, and agroecology. He focuses on the One Health aspect of regenerative agriculture with one goal in mind: healthy environment, healthy animals, healthy humans. More specifically, he is interested in crop-livestock integrated systems, the effects of environmental conditions (e.g., air quality) on animal welfare, and using biotechnology to facilitate high tannin-containing plants digestion in cattle. Dr. Krusinski also uses his data to better authenticate grass-finished beef to protect ranchers and consumers from food fraud.

title: Lucas Krusinski
content:

B.S., Nutritional Sciences, Morgan State University (2019)

title: Lucas Krusinski
content:
### **Overview**

The research in the Krusinski Lab is performed at the nexus of food and animal science. We investigate the effects of agricultural practices on meat nutrient density and authenticity. The Krusinski Lab performs high-throughput multi-omics work to measure fatty acids, vitamins, minerals, secondary metabolites, and animal health biomarkers in beef. Most of the work is performed on grass-finished cattle, and we are deeply interested in the use of supplemental feeds and agricultural by-products to enhance meat nutrient density and animal welfare. **We love solving rancher-identified issues, so if you are a rancher and would like to collaborate, please contact us!**

title: Linchi Kwok
content:
## About Dr. Kwok

Appointments are not required to meet during office hours, but highly encouraged on: <https://linchikwok.clickbook.net> (M - mostly in-person; Th - mostly online).

[Dr. Linchi Kwok](https://www.linkedin.com/in/linchikwok/) is a Top 2% Scientist and a full professor in the Collins College of Hospitality Management at California State Polytechnic University, Pomona (Cal Poly Pomona), where he exemplifies the university’s teacher-scholar model. Dr. Kwok has distinguished himself through high-impact scholarly contributions to the global hospitality community, innovative research and teaching approaches, and exceptional citizenship within the organizations he serves.
In 2024, Dr. Kwok became the first faculty member at his college to receive the [Provost Award for Excellence in Scholarly and Creative Activities](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FJ061ibykek) at Cal Poly Pomona.

[Dr. Kwok’s research](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=VhdAIRkAAAAJ&view_op=list_works) interests include hospitality management, service marketing, and information technology. Between 2010 and 2024, he submitted more than 30 grant applications and received more than $170,000 in internal and external funding to support his scholarly work. His research often appears in top-tier journals, including the *International Journal of Hospitality Management (IJHM), International Journal of Contemporary Hospitality Management (IJCHM), Journal of Hospitality and Tourism Research (JHTR),*and *Cornell Hospitality Quarterly (CHQ),*and has produced several highly cited and downloaded papers. Reflecting the quality of his work, he serves on the editorial boards of *IJHM*(corresponding editor)*, IJCHM, JHTR,*and*CHQ.*

Beyond academia, Dr. Kwok is widely recognized as a thought leader in hospitality and tourism. He regularly contributes content to media outlets, including *[HospitalityNet.org](https://www.hospitalitynet.org/author/148003349.html),*the No. 1-ranked website for the global hospitality community. His insights are regularly circulated through email newsletters to professional organizations. In addition to TV and radio interviews, his perspectives have been quoted in mainstream outlets such as *The LA Times,**Condé Nast Traveler, National Geographic,*and*NBC News.*

Dr. Kwok’s scholarly and creative activities have also provided students with immersive learning experiences. He was recognized as a COOL (Celebrating Outstanding Opportunities for Learning) Professor at Cal Poly Pomona in 2014. He developed and led a study abroad program in Thailand. He developed and taught a “Learn through Discovery: Signature Polytechnic Experiences (PolyX)” class, advising student teams to compete in the CoStar (STR) Global Student Market Study Competition. His student teams have won numerous prizes in the competition since their debut in 2019.

Dr. Kwok is an exceptional team player. He has developed and taught more than 20 new undergraduate and graduate courses for the Collins College of Hospitality Management and the Singelyn Graduate School of Business at Cal Poly Pomona. During Cal Poly Pomona’s quarter-to-semester conversion, Dr. Kwok led the college's Curriculum Committee in restructuring its curriculum. His tenure as Chair of Collins’ Retention, Tenure, and Promotion (RTP) Committee between 2019 and 2021 was marked by significant revisions to the College’s RTP document (2020/21 - 2024/25). He then served on the University RTP Committee between 2022 and 2024. He was a Senator and a member of the Executive Committee of the University's Academic Senate from 2019 to 2022.

Before joining Cal Poly Pomona, Dr. Kwok was a tenure-track faculty member of hospitality management at Syracuse University (SU), where he was named a Kauffman Professor in Entrepreneurship and Innovation (2011 – 2013) to support the university’s initiatives in teaching and research excellence in entrepreneurship and innovation. In addition to his tenured and tenure-track positions, Dr. Kwok has taught at Rochester Institute of Technology and Texas Tech University (TTU). Internationally, he is now a visiting professor at the Faculty of Liberal Arts at Rajamangala University of Technology Thanyaburi (RMUTT) in Thailand and was a visiting professor at the AACSB-, EQUIS-, and AMBA-accredited Sun Yet Sen Business School from 2017 to 2021.

Dr. Kwok holds an M.S. and a Ph.D. degree in Hospitality Administration from TTU and an MBA degree from SU. His professional portfolio also includes several years of managerial and hands-on experience in the hospitality industry in the U.S. and mainland China.

title: Lianlian Lin
content:
## Bio

Dr. Lianlian Lin is a Professor of Management at Cal Poly Pomona. She received her Ph.D. in Business Administration from the University of Texas at Austin, and LL.M. degree from University of Pennsylvania Law School.  She teaches principles of management, international management, and senior projects. Her student donated a scholarship in honor of her. Dr. Lin has done interdisciplinary research and cross-cultural studies on business management, economics, finance, and law.  She published her research not only in business and economics journals but also in legal journals such as *Yale Journal of International Law**,* and *University of Pennsylvania Journal of International Economic Law*. She wrote case studies for global business textbooks and served as a reviewer for management textbooks and international business and management conferences.

Dr. Lin is frequently invited to give seminars and speeches about management, leadership, education and talent development. She has served as the president of a few professional and community organizations, including President of Professors and Experts Federation USA, and CEO of Roundtable of Chinese American Organizations. She is a recipient of several outstanding leadership awards, service awards, and achievement awards. Her profile is included in several well-known Who’s Who books in the U.S. and in the world.

title: Laura Epperson
content:
## About Me

Laura Epperson is a teaching artist, performer, and community collaborator. Through the Performing Justice Project (PJP), she devises original performances with youth artists that explore the intersection of identity, power, and (in)justice. She has co-directed projects with teens in foster care in Austin (TX), youth theatre board members in Boston (MA), and young adults with disabilities in Warsaw (Poland).

Laura has worked with the Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts (DC), Drama for Schools (TX), and Creative Arts Team (NY) to develop arts-full approaches to teaching and learning across curricula and age range. She’s led social justice arts residences at over 20 New York City public schools and facilitated professional learning intensives for educators in Australia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Taiwan, and throughout the United States.

Laura has taught and appeared as a guest artist-lecturer at Virginia Tech, UT Austin, James Madison University, and Austin Community College. She received the American Alliance for Theare and Education (AATE) Distinguished Thesis Award for her research on the aesthetics of healing in PJP. Laura frequently presents her practice-based work at professional conferences including National Guild for Community Arts Educators, International Teaching Artist Collaborative, Pedagogy and Theatre of the Oppressed, and AATE.

Laura has a B.S. in Theatre Performance from University of Evansville and an M.F.A. in Drama and Theatre for Youth and Communities from University of Texas, Austin. She is thrilled to collaborate with CPP students to use storytelling, embodiment, play, and inquiry to create community-engaged project-performances that imagine justice and joyful futures.

title: Lorena Garcia
content:
## About Me

Lorena Garcia is a curator, educator and founder of PUENTEALAMEDA, an international landscape architecture firm with base in Los Angeles and Madrid.

Majored in Forest Engineering (USC, Spain). In 2004 she moved to Madrid and completed her studies, earning a Master of Landscape Architecture (UPM, Spain). She also managed to complete and coordinate a postgraduate course on the “Restoration of Historic Gardens, Parks, and Cultural Landscapes” (ETSAM, Madrid). Over the course of the next 6 years she collaborated with various world-awarded architecture firms, including: Acebo X Alonso (Premio Nacional Jovenes Arquitectos), MTM Arquitectos, Langarita-Navarro (Mies van der Rohe Award), Manuel Ocana and Flast Arquitectura.

In 2011 she founded PuenteAlameda Landscape Architecture Research. A firm that explores creative and collaborative designs, addressing the complexity of the nature processes, with projects as Reina Sofia Museum Master Plan (Madrid, Spain), Hotel Kamaroti (Sifnos, Greece), MediaLab-Prado Gardens (Madrid) and Allonbay Green Urban Development (Alicante, Spain).

She has been a lecturer at the Landscape Architecture Department, College of Environmental Design (CPP, CA); and at Madrid School of Design (Spain); assistant of professor Ana Luengo PhD in European University of Madrid, and coordinator of post graduate courses in Polytechnic University of Madrid.

title: Lee-Anne S Milburn
content:
## About Me

Lee-Anne Milburn, Ph.D.,  is a full professor in the Department of Landscape Architecture. She has a Bachelor's degree (honors) in Fine Arts (University of Manitoba), Master's in Landscape Architecture (University of Guelph), and a Ph.D. in Rural Studies: Environmental Design and Rural Development (University of Guelph). I'm licensed as a Landscape Architect in the State of North Carolina.

She has taught in institutions throughout the United States, among them Mississippi State University, North Carolina State University, and University of Nevada Las Vegas.

Dr. Milburn holds the Carpenter Medal for National Excellence in Teaching. In the past, she has won both a Canadian (CSLA) and an American (ASLA) Research Award, and with her students won an ASLA Community Service Award.

Her passions are sustainability (especially water and air quality) and therapeutic design (design for health).

title: Laurie S. Starkey
content:
## About Me

**Professor, teaching Organic Chemistry lecture and laboratory classes.**In addition to authoring an introductory organic synthesis [textbook](http://www.amazon.com/dp/0470484098/ref%3Drdr_ext_tmb) ([2nd edition](https://www.wiley.com/en-us/Introduction%2Bto%2BStrategies%2Bfor%2BOrganic%2BSynthesis%2C%2B2nd%2BEdition-p-9781119347248)). I have developed online [lab animations & tutorials](https://www.chemistryconnected.com/ochemlab/index.html), online prelab quizzes, organic chemistry "[clicker](https://www.chemistryconnected.com/local/index.html)" questions, and [online organic chemistry lectures](https://www.educator.com/chemistry/organic-chemistry/starkey/) for Educator.com. As you can probably tell, I am a strong proponent for using [technology](https://www.chemistryconnected.com/references.html) to support student success, and I have created a repository of active learning resources for Organic Chemistry, called [LOCAL](https://www.chemistryconnected.com/local/index.html). I served as a peer reviewer for [MERLOT](http://www.merlot.org/), and I enjoy giving chemistry career [presentations](https://www.chemistryconnected.com/ChemCareers.pps). Please visit my YouTube channel [ChemistryConnected](http://www.youtube.com/user/ChemistryConnected), see my [OrganicERS](https://www.organicers.org/?q=?q=content/laurie-starkey-cal-poly-pomona) spotlight, and visit my collection of [Video Creation & Teaching resources](https://www.chemistryconnected.com/references.html) and [COVID-19 Remote-Teaching resources](https://www.chemistryconnected.com/covid19/index.html)

## Bio

Dr. Lin Tan is a Professor of Finance at Cal Poly Pomona. She publishes in the areas of international finance and behavioral finance. She teaches undergraduate and graduate courses in managerial finance, corporate finance, business forecasting, international finance, and financial markets and institutions. Before returning to graduate school, she worked in the international banking industry for six years.

title: L. Lin Ong
content:
## Bio

L. Lin Ong (Ph.D., University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill) is an Associate Professor of International Business and Marketing at the College of Business Administration, Cal Poly Pomona (California State Polytechnic University, Pomona). She is a US Fulbright Scholar, receiving a competitive award to research in Malaysia at the Asia School of Business (2024-25). Dr. Ong has published in the *Journal of Business Research, Journal of the Association of Consumer Research,*and *Journal of Consumer Affairs.*

Dr. Ong's research looks broadly at the interaction of business and society, with a focus on consumer vulnerability in digital and financial spaces. She is interested in increasing consumer well-being through effective marketing strategies and public policy. Dr. Ong is active in the [Transformative Consumer Research](https://www.mytcr.org/) movement, co-chairing the 2025 international conference hosted at American University (Washington, DC). Dr. Ong's doctoral dissertation analyzed the economic networks and decision-making of low-income female microentrepreneurs in coastal Kenya. She has lived, worked, or traveled on every continent except for Antarctica.

At CPP, Dr. Ong teaches in the fields of international business, international marketing, consumer behavior, and market research. As an educator, she is passionate about helping students hone their critical thinking skills and connect management theories to current events, increasing their ability to make effective business decisions.

Dr. Ong is a Faculty Advisor for the [Center for Customer Insights and Digital Marketing](https://www.cpp.edu/cba/customer-insights-lab/index.shtml) as well as Fulbright Liaison at CPP. Her past experience includes Director of New Professionals on the Board of the [Insights Association West Chapter](https://www.insightsassociation.org/Membership/Chapters/West), a market insights and analytics professional organization; Research Scholar in Data Innovation for [FP CAPE](https://www.fpcape.org/), a Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation funded global health project, focused on Nigeria and the DRC; and management consulting with [Deloitte Consulting's Strategy and Operations](https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/operations/solutions/strategy-and-operations-consulting.html) division.

title: Lin Wu, Ph.D.
content:
## About me

I am a Professor Emerita of Geography at Cal Poly Pomona. I received my Bachelor and Master degrees from East China Normal University and Ph.D. from UCLA. After 30 years teaching, I semi-retired in 2020. I now teach Fall semesters and off spring semesters. In 30 years, I moved through the academic ranks to full professor and served as the department chair for six years. In addition, I served as an adjunct faculty for the Regenerative Studies for 20 years. My teaching and research are in the areas of Climatology, Physical Geography, Geographic Information Science (GIS), Environmental Modeling, and Environmental Sustainability.

title: Lydia M. Chen Shah
content:
## About Me

Lydia Chen Shah is a leader in strategy, sustainability, brand/product management, integrated marketing communications, and organizational culture. As a professor at Cal Poly Pomona in the International Business and Marketing department, she has collaborated with numerous community and industry partners where students gain real-world polytechnic experience. Deeply committed to education of the whole person and service learning, Lydia was selected by CPP’s Center for Community Engagement as a Faculty Fellow in 2015 and by Campus Compact as an Engaged Scholar in 2019.

As a consultant, Lydia has worked with clients in social impact, education, lifestyle, and technology sectors. Her professional experience spans from startups to Fortune 100 companies. She also serves on the executive committee for Cal Poly Pomona’s Alumni Association board of directors.

With a passion for equity-driven education, Lydia focuses on providing students the best possible experiences in and outside of the classroom. This includes real-world application of knowledge and developing relevant skills desired by employers. She is a leader in strategy, sustainability, brand/product management, integrated marketing communications, and organizational culture. Lydia's industry experience ranging from startups to Fortune companies plus community-based organizations turns diverse perspectives into actionable plans. She fosters excellence in others through a culture of collaboration. Lydia also serves on Cal Poly Pomona’s Alumni Association board of directors and as a Faculty Associate for CPP's Career Center.

title: Mark A. Generous
content:
## About Me

Mark Generous (Ph.D., Arizona State University, 2016) is an Assistant Professor of Communication. He teaches in the Interpersonal Communication option.

Professor Generous’s research focuses on challenging conversations in close, intimate relationships. He has researched how romantic couples talk about sexual intimacy and affection, how families engage in end-of-life communication with terminally ill family members, and how students and teachers develop relationships in the classroom. He received his B.A. (Broadcast and Cinematic Arts; Communication) from Central Michigan University and M.A. (Communication) from Texas State University.

***Professor Generous teaches:***

* Communication Theory
* Communication Research
* Interpersonal Communication
* Advanced Communication Research
* Family Communication
* Nonverbal Communication

title: Adaickapillai Mahendran
content:
## Research Interest

Our group’s research interest folds into two areas: a) Developing novel histone deacetylase 6 (HDAC6) selective enzyme inhibitors for cancer treatment and b) Understanding the chemistry, biological effects, and toxicity profile of oxidation products of phytocannabinoids.

**Development of novel HDAC6 selective inhibitors:**

HDAC6 has been identified as a potential target for cancer therapy. Current FDA-approved HDAC inhibitors, including suberoylanilide hydroxamic acid (SAHA), Panobinostat, and Belinostat contain hydroxamic acid functionality to chelate co-enzyme Zn2+ ion. Strong chelation of this hydroxamate group leads these drugs to be non-selective and toxic. Thiohydroxamic acid is a compound similar to hydroxamic acid, but its chelation properties and selectivity profiles are not fully explored. In our lab, we synthesize, characterize, and study the metal binding properties of model thiohydroxamic acid molecules. Then we study its bioactivity against HDAC enzymes and its selectivity profile with collaboration. Our long-term goal is to develop novel HDAC6 selective inhibitors (eg.  **thio-HPOB**) similar to known compounds **HPOP** and **HPB**.

**Oxidation of phytocannabinoids and its biological effects:**

Phytocannabinoids, including delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinol (Δ9-THC) and cannabidiol (CBD), are well known for their medicinal uses. It is also known that with prolonged exposure of these cannabinoids to sunlight, they lose their properties as they get oxidized. Cannabidiolquinone (CBDQ) is one such oxidized compound inclined to undergo further reactions with nucleophiles. It is not fully understood the biological effects of these quinone intermediates and their addition products. We are interested in the chemical oxidation process of phytocannabinoids, specifically THC and CBD. We are also interested in the mechanism of this oxidation process, supported by DFT calculations. With our biology collaborators, we study their bioactivity against different enzymes and cell-lines.

title: Maria Ortiz
content:
## Bio

Dr. Maria J. Ortiz is an Assistant Professor of Marketing at the International Business and Marketing (IBM) Department in the College of Business Administration at Cal Poly Pomona. She has experience teaching undergraduate courses, including Digital Marketing, International Marketing, and Introduction to Marketing, as well as graduate courses, including Online Consumer Psychology and Behaviors.

She is an experimental researcher who focuses on consumer behavior in the areas of socially responsible behavior, online decision-making, and joint consumption. Her research has contributed to the understanding of consumption amount and subscription decisions. Her work has been published in the Psychology & Marketing journal. Her research has been presented at various conferences, including the Association for Consumer Research and the Society for Consumer Psychology.

Before becoming an academic, she was a Project Manager in the Division of Business and Economic Research at the University of New Orleans. She worked on practical research on customer profiles, economic impacts, and market analyses for the New Orleans area and the state of Louisiana. She obtained her Ph.D. in Business Administration - Marketing from Concordia University in Montreal and her Bachelor's and MBA from the University of New Orleans.

She is originally from Ecuador and is fluent in Spanish. In her spare time, she enjoys traveling with her family.

title: Jeff Marshall
content:
## About Me

Dr. Jeff Marshall is a Professor of Geological Sciences and Regenerative Studies at Cal Poly Pomona. He is a geomorphologist with research and teaching expertise in geologic hazards, coastal processes, and watershed systems. Much of his research focuses on the neotectonics and landscape evolution of active plate margins, with over 30 years of experience conducting fieldwork in California, Mexico, Central America, Australia, and New Zealand. Dr. Marshall studied geology and earth surface processes at U.C. Santa Barbara (BA), U.C. Santa Cruz (MS), and Penn State (PhD).

For the past three decades, Dr. Marshall has engaged in the research and education efforts of the National Science Foundation MARGINS, GeoPRISMS, and SZ4D programs, including funded coastal tectonics research in Costa Rica and New Zealand, serving on the GeoPRISMS Education Advisory Committee, and leading field trips, workshops, and student research symposia, both in the U.S. and abroad. He has substantial experience mentoring international undergraduate field research with the Keck Geology Consortium and his own NSF REU projects. Dr. Marshall is a seven-term Geosciences Councilor with the Council on Undergraduate Research (CUR), and was the original Cal Poly Pomona Undergraduate Research Coordinator, leading efforts to establish the Kellogg Undergraduate Scholars Program (KUSP), Undergraduate Research Faculty Advisory Council (URFAC), and campus Office of Undergraduate Research (CPP-OUR). He now serves on the California State University Council on Ocean Affairs, Science, and Technology (CSU COAST), and is a faculty participant in the Cal Poly Pomona - Air Force Research Lab (AFRL) Educational Partnership for Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles Technology. In 2018, Dr. Marshall received the Ralph W. Ames Distinguished Research Award from the Cal Poly Pomona College of Science.
