title: Fearless Classroom®
content:
# Fearless Classroom®

A Fearless Classroom is an intentional approach that enables student academic performance, engagement in the classroom, and commitment to their education from a psychologically safe classroom community that is inclusive and equitable where there is mutual trust and respect between the instructor and the students, among the students, and where the instructor demonstrates empathy, compassion, and humility toward the students.

As of 2024, 92% of students agreed their instructor created a comfortable (fearless) learning environment that supported their ability to succeed in the course (n=13,361).

![2022 Assessment Revolutionary Award ](../img/awards/fearless-awards-assessment-revolutionary-award-.png)

title: Fearless Classroom®
content:
## 2022 Assessment Revolutionary Award

View Award

title: Fearless Classroom®
content:
### 2022 Assessment Revolutionary Award

×

![2022 Assessment Revolutionary Award ](../img/awards/fearless-awards-assessment-revolutionary-award-.png)

This award celebrates the exceptional efforts of Cal Poly Pomona faculty who improve student learning and the learning environment by developing and implementing closing-the-loop strategies and changes (to teaching, curriculum, or instructional design) in response to assessment data at the program, college, or institutional level.

The selection committee carefully reviewed your application and was thrilled to see how faculty improved the learning environment for students by the creation of fearless classrooms. The fact that this project transcends a single program, extending its reach across the university is to be particularly commended.

Congratulations to all the fearless faculty at CPP!

Close

![One Team Award](../img/awards/fearless-awards-oneteam.png)

title: Fearless Classroom®
content:
## 2022 One Team Winners

View Award

title: Fearless Classroom®
content:
### One Team Award

×

![award winners group photo](../img/awards/2022-fearless-campus-award-winner.jpg)

The committee received a crowd of deserving nominees, including Fearless Classroom who is going to extraordinary lengths to create a more inclusive and psychologically safe environment for all members of the CPP Community" (President Coley, Fall Convocation, 2018)

title: Fearless Classroom®
content:
## Fearless Classroom Conceptual Model

The Fearless Classroom conceptual module presents attributes on the left side of the triangle that create a psychologically safe (fearless) learning environment for students that enable student academic performance, commitment to their education and path to graduation. The right side of the triangle represents attributes that create fear that can impede student academic performance, commitment to their education, and path to graduation. The white space at the top of the triangle represents barriers to a fearless classroom environment. The animation illustrates how increasing the fearless attributes drive out the fearful attributes creating a fearless classroom.

title: Fearless Facts
content:
# Fearless Facts

![](../img/logo/fcdataproven.png)

* 54,682 students enrolled in courses where the professor attended a Fearless Classroom training since January 2020
* 16,801 students in enrolled in courses identified as Fearless Classrooms by the professor since January 2020
* 11,518 students participating in the end of semester exit surveys since May 2020
* 20 Fearless Classroom workshops since November 2019
* 302 faculty attended Fearless Classroom workshops since November 2019
* 2,588 Fearless Classrooms created since January 2020
* 292 faculty implemented Fearless Classroom concepts in their courses since January 2020
* 8 colleges and the university library represented in the Fearless Classroom project since January 2020
* 43 college departments represented in the Fearless Classroom project since January 2020

As of 2024, 92% of students agreed their instructor created a comfortable (fearless) learning environment that supported their ability to succeed in the course (n=13,361).

title: Fearless Faculty™
content:
# Fearless Faculty™

Fearless Faculty intentionally create psychologically safe classroom environments that enable student academic performance by modelling resiliency and vulnerability, providing structure and clarity in course objectives to the students while building mutual trust and respect with the students, demonstrate compassion, empathy, and humility toward the students.

title: Fearless Faculty™
content:
## Fearless Faculty Testimonials

The Fearless Classroom strategies directly affect our student's Social Emotional Learning. Coming back to school and campus, students are having a difficult time adapting and adjusting to the expectations being placed on them. Using FC strategies support the students social emotional learning and well-being by providing students with authentic, trusting, and collaborative relationships that still holds students accountable without reducing the rigor. I would also go as far as discussing how research shows that SEL has a positive impact on several outcomes, not just academic performance but healthy relationships and mental health and wellness. (Marybeth Murray, Early Childhood Studies)

---

Thank you for being our 'fearless' leaders this past year.  I am grateful for all I have gleaned from the workshops as I feel changes to my classes have been positive.  I wanted to share that next fall I am committing two of my classes to an ‘un-grading' system.  This is based on the CAFE workshops, trainings and texts.  I am very excited (and scared) as I believe this is directly related to creating a fearless classroom.  I see great benefits for creating a class of equity and psychological safety as well as student learning centered. (Jeanne Marshall, Geography/Anthropology)

---

During the pandemic I created more balance in my teaching approach building a community out of a classroom, working with students as a class, in teams, and individually. I noticed more engagement, better attendance, and increased performance. I was awarded Teacher of the Year in my department. (Ron Heimler, Apparel Merchandising and Management)

---

I was stunned when I calculated the final grades - almost everyone in my class got an A or an A-. All students regardless of grade worked harder and, I think, learned better. Their final projects are much better than last year in the same class. I actually made some of them revise their first submissions.. Even though it was more work for me and the students, I think the opportunity to recover paid off. Their Discussion Board posts were more insightful measuring in 100 - 300 words by the end fo the semester rather than a single sentence or two. Even their responses to the takeaways discussion in Week 15 were rewarding to me reflecting they learned something important that they are taking away beyond the classroom. (Olga Griswold, Linguistics and TESOL, English and Modern Languages

---

I really appreciate this project, it has helped enhance my work as an instructor.

---

Thank you and your team for the consistent dedication to the Fearless process in education.  This program has brought me to a place where I feel my teaching philosophy and what is happening in the classroom have come together. Thank you for stretching me in this direction.

---

 I have learned a lot about Fearless Classroom and absolutely think all faculty should take this session. It was insightful and amazing.

title: Fearless Leaders
content:
# Fearless Leaders

Fearless Leadership is an intentional approach to institutionalize a Fearless Campus culture from a psychologically safe campus environment that enables performance and retention where university leaders build trust and respect with all stakeholders and where the leadership demonstrates empathy, compassion, and humility toward all stakeholders. internal and external.

Creating a fearless environment is the responsibility of every individual working collectively toward creating a collaborative Environment where people feel included, safe to learn, safe to contribute, and safe to challenge the status quo respectfully. The formal leader in the room has the responsibility to go first and is accountable for the outcome. The following images illustrate these ideas.

![Fearless leaders](../img/graphics/fearless-leaders.png)

title: Fearless Leaders
content:
## Fearless Leaders

This image, first created by Nordstrom, reminds leaders at all levels including staff that the customer comes first resonates with the Fearless Campus that it is the student that comes first.

The president found at the bottom of the pyramid is ultimately accountable and carries the burden to support the entire campus with each ascending leadership level supporting the levels above to ultimately serve the students.

![](../img/animated/fearless-leaders.gif)

title: Fearless Leaders
content:
## Fearless Leader Conceptual Model

The Fearless Leader conceptual module represents attributes on the left side of the triangle that create a psychologically safe (fearless) leadership environment that enables leader performance and retention at all levels within the university. The right side of the triangle represents attributes that create fears that can impede leader performance and retention. The white space at the top of the triangle represents barriers to a fearless leader environment. The animation illustrates how fearless attributes drive out fearful attributes creating a fearless leader environment.

content:

title: Articles
content:
## [7 Tasks Every Leader Must Master](docs/7-tasks-every-leader-must-master.pdf)

**By Chris Mayfield • Entrepreneur • August 8, 2022**

7 Tasks Every Leader Must Master: This important article highlights a number of the qualities emphasized in a Fearless Campus and a Fearless Classroom: the climate, trust, respect, empathy, and humility

![article thumbnail](img/fc-article-inc-how-can.png)

title: Articles
content:
## [How Can You Be Sure Someone Has Exceptional Leadership Skills?](docs/how-can-you-be-sure-someone.pdf)

**By Marcel Schwantes • INC • August 6, 2022**

Communication skills typically include oral and written communication skills however listening, most importantly being an empathetic active listener, is a critical leadership skill. This article focuses on 2 important words "active listening" as part of being empathetic

![article thumbnail](img/fc-article-uknews-humility.png)
section: [How Can You Be Sure Someone Has Exceptional Leadership Skills?](docs/how-can-you-be-sure-someone.pdf)

title: Articles
content:
## [Humility is a virtue. But can humble people be successful in the modern world?](docs/humility-is-a-virtue.pdf)

**By Lena Burton • UK Daily News • October 5, 2022**

Humility Is a Virtue. But Can Humble People Succeed in the Modern World? see attached for the full article. The link does not show the full article as it requires NY Times access.  Here's my introduction:

Philosophers consider humility to be a virtue. In the modern world we may find it to be an important quality and even more so in leading in a fearless environment. The author's contrast of capitalism in Adam's Smith mind and business today is interesting- Who wants to do business with a jerk? Who wants to work for or with a jerk as described in the article. Being humble, being respected and respectful, building trust and being trusted, in essence being humble is a virtue for success.

![](img/fc-article-dei.jpg)
section: [Humility is a virtue. But can humble people be successful in the modern world?](docs/humility-is-a-virtue.pdf)

title: Articles
content:
## [DEI Gets Real](docs/dei-gets-real.pdf)

**By Dagny Dukach · Harvard Business Review · January–February 2022**

It’s easy to view the corporate world’s growing emphasis on DEI—diversity, equity, and inclusion—with cynicism. Too often attempts to address discrimination seem to be more about optics than about real change, with business leaders’ being quick to issue statements of support but sluggish when it comes to taking meaningful action. And if you’ve ever rolled your eyes at a buzzword-laden corporate diversity training, you’re not alone...[Continue Reading]

![](img/fc-article-empathy-is.jpg)

title: Articles
content:
## [Empathy Is The Most Important Leadership Skill According To Research](docs/empathy-is-the-most-important-leadership-skill-according-to-research.pdf)

**By Tracy Brower · Forbes · Sep 19, 2021**

Empathy has always been a critical skill for leaders, but it is taking on a new level of meaning and priority. Far from a soft approach it can drive significant business results. [Continue Reading]

![](img/fc-article-the-surprising-thing.jpg)
section: [Empathy Is The Most Important Leadership Skill According To Research](docs/empathy-is-the-most-important-leadership-skill-according-to-research.pdf)

title: Articles
content:
## [The surprising thing Google learned about its employees — and what it means for today’s students](docs/the-surprising-thing-google-learned-about-its-employees--and-what-it-means-for-todays-students.pdf)

The conventional wisdom about 21st century skills holds that students need to master the STEM subjects — science, technology, engineering and math — and learn to code as well because that’s where the jobs are. It turns out that is a gross simplification of what students need to know and be able to do, and some proof for that comes from a surprising source: Google. [continue reading...]

![](img/fc-article-youtube-how-google.jpg)
section: [The surprising thing Google learned about its employees — and what it means for today’s students](docs/the-surprising-thing-google-learned-about-its-employees--and-what-it-means-for-todays-students.pdf)

title: Bookshelf
content:
# Bookshelf

title: Bookshelf
content:
## [The Fearless Organization](https://www.amazon.com/Fearless-Organization-Psychological-Workplace-Innovation/dp/1119477247/ref%3Dsr_1_1?crid=UQMAZTJM7BKX&keywords=the+fearless+organization+by+amy+edmondson&qid=1647386489&s=books&sprefix=the+fearless%2Cstripbooks%2C104&sr=1-1)

The Fearless Organization: Creating Psychological Safety in the Workplace for Learning, Innovation, and Growth

The Fearless Organization: Creating Psychological Safety in the Workplace for Learning, Innovation, and Growth offers practical guidance for teams and organizations to create psychologically safe environments where there is trust and respect allowing people to speak without fear enables performance and retention.

![Nonviolent Communication](img-book-cover/bc-nonviolent-communication.jpg)
